name: Elder
description: The Elder, or Elderberry (Sambucus nigra), holds a significant place in European folklore and tradition. Revered for its protective and healing properties, the Elder was often seen as a sacred tree, inhabited by the Elder Mother, also known as Hyldemoer in Scandinavian folklore. Respect for the tree was paramount; permission was often sought before cutting or using any part of it. The Elder was believed to ward off evil spirits, protect against witchcraft, and bring good fortune. Planting an Elder tree near a home was thought to safeguard the inhabitants from harm. Its branches were sometimes hung over doorways or windows for protection. Medicinally, the Elder has been used for centuries to treat colds, flu, and other ailments. The berries and flowers are rich in antioxidants and are believed to boost the immune system. The Elder's association with death and rebirth is also evident in some traditions. It was sometimes planted on graves, symbolizing the cyclical nature of life. The tree's connection to the underworld and the fairy realm further enhanced its mystical reputation. In various rituals, Elder wood was used to create wands and other magical tools. The Elder's versatility, both practically and symbolically, cemented its position as a cherished and powerful plant in European culture. The Elder Mother was considered a powerful being, capable of both blessing and cursing those who interacted with her tree. Proper respect and offerings were often given to appease her and ensure a positive outcome. Even today, the Elder continues to be appreciated for its beauty, medicinal properties, and rich cultural heritage.
subtitle: Sacred protector, healer, and guardian.

keyMyths.title: The Elder Mother
keyMyths.description: Hyldemoer, or the Elder Mother, is believed to reside within the Elder tree and protects it. Disrespecting the tree could incur her wrath, while treating it with reverence could bring blessings and good fortune.

keyMyths.title: Elder and Witchcraft
keyMyths.description: Elder was thought to be both a protection against and a tool for witchcraft. Some believed that witches could transform into Elder trees, while others used Elder wood in their magical practices.

sources.author: Jacob Grimm
sources.description: Explores the role of the Elder tree in Germanic folklore and mythology.
sources.text: Explores the role of the Elder tree in Germanic folklore and mythology.

sources.author: Margaret Baker
sources.description: Details the folklore and traditions associated with various plants, including the Elder.
